items.title: Toast.Root may remain mounted indefinitely when clicked while closing

labels.name: component: toast
labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

items.body:
When a toast is closing, it can become visually transparent while still mounted for the exit transition. If the user quickly clicks or interacts with that closing toast, the toast may remain in the DOM indefinitely.

This leaves an invisible element over the page, which can block clicks or other pointer interactions on content underneath.

Expected: a closing toast should still be removed after its exit transition finishes, even if the user interacts with it during the closing state.

items.title: [toast] queue added toasts when there is no listener

labels.name: status: waiting for maintainer
labels.description: These issues haven't been looked at yet by a maintainer.

labels.name: component: toast
labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

items.body:
# Feature request

## Summary

<!-- Provide a clear and concise description of the component or enhancement that you want. -->
This is probably a bit niche but we use the Toast component in a project where we use react-islands, which means that the `Toast.Provider` might not have been rendered when a toast is added to the global manager. When this is the case, the toast never reaches the store and thus never appears, even after the "Toaster" island has rendered.

Im thinking a solution is to add toasts to a queue when there is no listeners, and when a listener is added dump the queue into it, and skip the queue afterwards.

This is doable by writing a small wrapper, but I think it would be nice to have builtin!

If this is something you'd like to support, if so, let me know and I'll (try to, atleast) help however I can 😄 


## Examples in other libraries

<!-- Provide a link to examples of this component or feature implemented in other libraries (if applicable).  -->
n/a?

## Motivation

<!-- What are you trying to accomplish? Which kind of user is this for? Providing context helps us come up with a solution that is more useful in the real world -->
The motivation for me, is to be able to add toasts before the `ToastManager` has received any subscribers, which our island/adhoc-toaster-rendering-system would "need".

items.title: [toast] Fix timer and limit edge cases

labels.name: type: bug
labels.description: It doesn't behave as expected.

labels.name: component: toast
labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

items.body:
Toast now handles several limit, timer, and focus edge cases that could leave hidden or inactive toasts in the wrong state.

## Changes

- Recomputed limited toast state when the provider `limit` changes.
- Shared limited-state recomputation across add, close, and provider sync paths.
- Reset paused timer state when all active timers are cleared so future toasts can pause and resume normally.
- Only prevented touch scrolling while a toast swipe is active.
- Kept timers paused while the window is blurred, even if mouseleave or blur handlers run.
- Skipped ending and limited toasts when focus enters the viewport.
- Updated Toast docs and API output for the current limit and manager behavior.
- Added regression coverage for Toast root, viewport, content, manager, and store behavior.

items.title: [toast] Remove memoized selector

labels.name: component: toast
labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

items.body:
Removes Toast's memoized selector use while keeping the ID-based toast metadata lookup fast.

## Changes

- Store toast metadata directly when the toast list changes.
- Keep toast metadata synchronized through close transitions.
- Leave `createSelectorMemoized` and `reselect` in utils for other consumers.

## Performance

Local Node microbenchmark comparing upstream/master to this PR with 50 toasts:

| Operation | upstream/master | PR |
| --- | ---: | ---: |
| Metadata map rebuild after toast list change | 1.864 us/op | 1.100 us/op |
| Cached selector read by id | 0.017 us/op | 0.007 us/op |

The rebuild timing moved: upstream rebuilds lazily on the first selector read after a toast list change, while this PR rebuilds when the toast list is written. Toast lists are normally small in practice, so this is not a meaningful cost.

items.title: [toast] Fix stuck drag state on release

labels.name: component: toast
labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

items.body:
This fixes a toast drag edge case where release cleanup can be missed after dragging a toast far in the direction other than that of the expected dismissal directions.

In practice, the toast can feel like it is still being controlled by the mouse after the press has already been released. In a stack, that same stale drag state can leave the card suspended out of place with its content hidden behind the frontmost toast.

The change keeps the active drag state in refs, listens for release/cancel events on the owner document for the lifetime of the gesture, and safely handles pointer capture APIs so release cleanup still runs.

Before:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/52b19912-c666-4bc5-93bf-5582a3368d32

After:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/48f46c30-6a48-47fd-bd6d-2c58d5e573e3

I tested this with:

- `pnpm test:jsdom toast --no-watch`
- `pnpm typescript --pretty false`

- [x] I have followed (at least) the [PR section of the contributing guide](https://github.com/mui/base-ui/blob/HEAD/CONTRIBUTING.md#sending-a-pull-request).

items.title: [toast] Add focus option to move focus to toast on open

labels.name: PR: out-of-date
labels.description: The pull request has merge conflicts and can't be merged.

labels.name: component: toast
labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

items.body: ## Summary Adds a `focus` option to `toastManager.add()` that moves focus to the toast when it opens. This enables keyboard and screen reader users to reach interactive actions (e.g. Undo) without relying on the F6 shortcut. Closes #4253 ## Changes - **`useToastManager.ts`** — Add `focus?: boolean` to `ToastObject` - **`ToastRoot.tsx`** — On mount, if `focus` is `true`: save `prevFocusElement`, focus the toast, pause timers, and set `focused` state (same pattern as F6 in `ToastViewport`) - **`ToastRoot.test.tsx`** — Add tests for focus behavior - **Undo demo** — Apply `focus: true` to demonstrate the pattern ## How it works ``` 1. toastManager.add({ focus: true, ... }) 2. Toast mounts → useEffect saves prevFocusElement, focuses the toast 3. Viewport's handleFocus pauses timers (existing infra) 4. User interacts (Undo/Close) or tabs away → timers resume (existing infra) 5. On close → handleFocusManagement restores focus (existing infra) ``` When `focus` is not set, behavior is identical to before — no breaking changes. ## Screenshot | Tab → Undo → Click | Tab → Undo → Tab out → auto-dismiss | |:---:|:---:| | | |

items.title: [toast] Add upsert support to add method

labels.name: type: new feature
labels.description: Expand the scope of the product to solve a new problem.

labels.name: component: toast
labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

items.body:
Closes #4228

https://deploy-preview-4440--base-ui.netlify.app/react/components/toast#deduplicated-toast

This adds built-in upsert behavior to `toastManager.add()` when callers pass an `id`, so repeated adds update the existing toast instead of creating a duplicate. Matched toasts keep their identity, refresh their auto-dismiss timer, and a toast that is already ending is replaced with a fresh one instead of being revived mid-dismissal.

It also adds `updateKey` to toast objects so custom renderers can replay styles when a toast is updated or upserted. The docs now include a deduplicated toast demo that uses `updateKey` to pulse the existing toast without remounting it.

## Changes

- Treat `add({ id })` as an upsert in the toast store and increment `updateKey` on updates and upserts.
- Add regression coverage for manager and hook flows, including timer refresh, re-adding while a toast is closing, and `updateKey` changes on `add()` and `update()`.
- Document the `id` upsert behavior and add a deduplicated toast demo that replays a pulse via `updateKey`.

items.title: [toast] Timers never resume after window regains focus

labels.name: type: bug
labels.description: It doesn't behave as expected.

labels.name: component: toast
labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

items.body:
## Summary

When the browser window loses focus while toasts are visible, timers are correctly paused via `handleWindowBlur`. However, when the window regains focus, `handleWindowFocus` in `ToastViewport` exits early without calling `store.resumeTimers()` or `store.setIsWindowFocused(true)`, causing toasts to remain visible indefinitely until manually dismissed.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Go to https://base-ui.com/react/components/toast
2. Trigger a toast
3. Before it auto-dismisses, switch to another window/tab
4. Switch back
5. The toast never auto-dismisses

**Expected:** Toast should dismiss after its remaining timeout once the window regains focus.

## Root cause

In `packages/react/src/toast/viewport/ToastViewport.tsx`, the `handleWindowFocus` handler:

```js
function handleWindowFocus(event: FocusEvent) {
  if (event.relatedTarget || getTarget(event) === win) {
    return;
  }
  // ...resumeTimers() and setIsWindowFocused(true) never reached
}
```

When the user returns to the window (alt-tab, clicking the browser), the focus event fires with `event.target === window`, hitting the `getTarget(event) === win` early return. As a result:

1. `isWindowFocused` stays `false`
2. `resumeTimers()` is never called
3. Toasts remain visible forever

The `event.relatedTarget` check alone is sufficient to filter intra-page focus moves without blocking the window-level refocus case.

## Fix

#4438

items.title: [toast] Fix timers not resuming after window regains focus

labels.name: component: toast
labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

items.body:
Closes #4439

## Summary

- The `handleWindowFocus` handler in `ToastViewport` had a guard (`getTarget(event) === win`) that caused an early return when the window itself was the focus target — which is exactly the case when alt-tabbing back to the browser.
- This prevented `resumeTimers()` and `setIsWindowFocused(true)` from being called, causing toasts to remain visible indefinitely after switching away and back.
- The `event.relatedTarget` check alone is sufficient to filter intra-page focus moves without blocking window-level refocus.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Open the [Toast demo](https://base-ui.com/react/components/toast)
2. Trigger a toast
3. Before it auto-dismisses, switch to another window (alt-tab)
4. Switch back
5. **Before fix:** toast never auto-dismisses
6. **After fix:** toast dismisses after its remaining timeout

## Test plan

- [x] Existing toast tests pass (`pnpm test:jsdom toast --no-watch` — 206 passed)
- [ ] Manual verification: trigger toast → alt-tab away → alt-tab back → toast dismisses

items.title: [toast] Keep viewport expanded while touch swiping

labels.name: type: bug
labels.description: It doesn't behave as expected.

labels.name: component: toast
labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

labels.name: mobile
labels.description: Targets mobile platform.

items.body:
Touch swiping on mobile could let the toast viewport collapse while the gesture was still active if a stray mouseleave fired underneath the interaction.

## Changes

- Keep the toast viewport expanded while an active touch swipe is in progress.
- Reuse the shared toast store state so timer pausing and viewport expansion stay in sync.
- Add regression coverage for the touch-swipe hold case and keep the existing swipe tests green.

items.title: [drawer][toast] Support logical values for swipeDirection

labels.name: component: drawer
labels.description: Changes related to the drawer component.

labels.name: component: toast
labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

items.body:
# Discussion

Both [Drawer](https://base-ui.com/react/components/drawer#DrawerRoot-swipeDirection) and [Toast](https://base-ui.com/react/components/toast#ToastRoot-swipeDirection) have a `swipeDirection` prop. At the time of writing, `swipeDirection` supports these values: `'up'`, `'down'`, `'left'`, and `'right'`.

Given that Base UI already offers a [DirectionProvider](https://base-ui.com/react/utils/direction-provider) component for implementing RTL UIs, would it make sense to support `'inline-start'` and `'inline-end'` values for the `swipeDirection` prop so users don't need to manage it themselves? We already support logical values on the [`side`](https://base-ui.com/react/components/popover#PopoverPositioner-side) prop of Positioner parts like `Popover.Positioner`.

items.title: [toast] accessibility: Undo action can't be reached in time with a keyboard or screen reader, or its timing adjusted

labels.name: docs
labels.description: Improvements or additions to the documentation.

labels.name: accessibility
labels.description: a11y

labels.name: component: toast
labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

items.body:
## Current behavior

The "Undo action" Toast example triggers an important Toast with an "undo" button inside it. **Keyboard and screen reader users can't undo the action because focus doesn't move to the toast notification**. 

This should be high priority to fix as it potentially blocks keyboard and screen reader users from being able to complete important actions. 

Potential WCAG failures:
* See [Understanding WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 2.1.1 Keyboard](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/keyboard.html)
* See [Understanding WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 2.2.1 Timing Adjustable](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/timing-adjustable.html)

## Expected behavior

Keyboard focus either needs to move to these Toast notifications or the toast needs to be the next tab stop, and the toast should persist until dismissed or focus moves back out of them.

## Reproducible example

To replicate

1. Navigate to [Toast: Undo action example](https://base-ui.com/react/components/toast#undo-action)
2. Using only a keyboard, activate the "perform action" button
3. Try to tab to the toast and activate the "Undo" button
4. Note the toast has been loaded at the end of the DOM and disappears before it can be easily reached 
5. Repeat the test using a screen reader with keyboard

## Base UI version

v1.2.0

## Which browser are you using?

Chrome

## Which OS are you using?

Mac OS

## Which assistive tech are you using (if applicable)?

Keyboard, VoiceOver

## Additional context

In this case, moving focus to the toast and back to the originating control on close (or near to it, if the originating control no longer exists) would be a good solution. The toast should remain until it has been intentionally dismissed.

items.title: [toast] useToastManager() causes unnecessary re-renders for action-only consumers

labels.name: type: new feature
labels.description: Expand the scope of the product to solve a new problem.

labels.name: component: toast
labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

items.body:
## Summary

Components that call `useToastManager()` solely to fire toasts (using `add`, `close`, `update`, `promise`) re-render on **every** toast state change — even though they never read the `toasts` array.

## Reproduction

```tsx
function SaveButton() {
  const { add } = Toast.useToastManager();
  return <button onClick={() => add({ title: 'Saved!' })}>Save</button>;
}
```

This component re-renders every time **any** toast is added, closed, or times out — anywhere in the app.

## Root cause

`useToastManager()` unconditionally calls `store.useState('toasts')`, which registers a `useSyncExternalStore` subscription. Every mutation produces a new array reference, so `Object.is(oldToasts, newToasts)` is always `false` and the component re-renders.

The action methods (`addToast`, `closeToast`, etc.) are stable references — arrow functions on the `ToastStore` class instance. But the subscription is registered before the caller destructures the return value, so destructuring only `{ add }` doesn't help.

## Expected behavior

Components that only need to **dispatch** toast actions should **not** re-render when toast state changes. Only components that **read** `toasts` (to display them) should subscribe and re-render.

## Proposed solution

Add a `useToastActions()` hook that returns only the stable action methods without subscribing to state.

PR: #4233

items.title: [toast] Add useToastActions hook to avoid unnecessary re-renders

Unclear, so far, it feels low priority since toast tends to be meant to use very carefuly: #4229.

items.title: [toast] Enable closing all toasts

labels.name: type: new feature
labels.description: Expand the scope of the product to solve a new problem.

labels.name: component: toast
labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

items.body:
<!-- Thanks so much for your PR, your contribution is appreciated! ❤️ -->

This PR adds a way to close all toasts at once.

The `close` API now accepts an optional `id`:
- `close(id)` closes a specific toast
- `close()` closes all active toasts

Alternatively, I considered introducing a separate `closeAll` API, but decided to keep the change minimal for the following reasons:

- Adding `closeAll` could require changes to `ToastManagerEvent` used by ` subscribe`. I was not sure whether it is a public API, and making changes to the `ToastManagerEvent` might be considered a breaking change (though that can be decoupled).
- Other libraries such as Sonner and Chakra expose the same behavior through a single `dismiss` API, where calling it without an argument dismisses all toasts.

- [x] I have followed (at least) the [PR section of the contributing guide](https://github.com/mui/base-ui/blob/HEAD/CONTRIBUTING.md#sending-a-pull-request).

items.title: [toast] flushSync error in v1.0.0-rc

labels.name: type: regression
labels.description: A bug, but worse, it used to behave as expected.

labels.name: component: toast
labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

items.body:
# Bug report

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e070e478-daf2-4b9e-9d1b-89c87ef20688

## Current behavior

When adding an anchored toast using Toast.Positioner with an anchor prop, React throws the following error:

`flushSync was called from inside a lifecycle method. React cannot flush when React is already rendering. Consider moving this call to a scheduler task or micro task.`

The error occurs at Toast.Root inside the toast rendering loop. This happens even when deferring the toastManager.add() call using setTimeout, queueMicrotask, requestAnimationFrame

## Expected behavior

Adding an anchored toast should not trigger a flushSync error.

## Reproducible example

[Link](https://stackblitz.com/edit/psxau6po?file=src%2FApp.tsx,package.json)

## Base UI version

v1.0.0-rc.0

## Which browser are you using?

Chrome

## Which OS are you using?

macOS

items.title: [toast] Fix multiple swipe directions on same axis

labels.name: type: bug
labels.description: It doesn't behave as expected.

labels.name: component: toast
labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

items.body:
Fixes https://github.com/mui/base-ui/issues/3389

Preview: https://stackblitz.com/edit/qaq73qg2?file=src%2FApp.tsx

items.body:
# Get help

## Ask a question

I am not sure if this is a bug, and not finding any documentations mention about this.

If I set `swipeDirection={['right', 'left']}` on `<Toast.Root/>`, only one direction (either right or left) will work. The other direction will "bounce back."

items.title: [toast] Recalculate content height when layout size is fixed

labels.name: type: bug
labels.description: It doesn't behave as expected.

labels.name: component: toast
labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

items.body:
<!-- Thanks so much for your PR, your contribution is appreciated! ❤️ -->

- [x] I have followed (at least) the [PR section of the contributing guide](https://github.com/mui/base-ui/blob/HEAD/CONTRIBUTING.md#sending-a-pull-request).

Fixes #3336

Since `.Content` and `.Root` are both fixed in height, `ResizeObserver` doesn't fire (in the aforementioned scenario) when new content gets inserted. A `MutationObserver` is needed to recalculate the height in response to content changes.

items.title: [toast] `height: 100%` on `Toast.Content` causes height to not re-calculate when promise is resolved

labels.name: type: regression
labels.description: A bug, but worse, it used to behave as expected.

labels.name: component: toast
labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

items.body: # Bug report ## Current behavior 1. Open the provided StackBlitz: https://stackblitz.com/edit/3fowjwdp-fesss1pp 2. Click the **Run promise** button 3. After the promise resolves, the toast content updates but the toast height does not adjust to fit the new description text 4. Oddly, clicking the close button causes the toast height to update without actually closing the toast https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1777ab08-2d9d-40e0-ba43-d02122ff4eea ## Expected behavior When the promise resolves and the toast content changes, the toast's height should automatically recalculate and resize to fit the new content. ## Reproducible example https://stackblitz.com/edit/3fowjwdp-fesss1pp ## Base UI version v1.0.0-beta.6 ## Which browser are you using? Chrome ## Which OS are you using? Mac OS ## Additional context In our app we were using `height: 100%` on `Toast.Content`, and after upgrading to beta.6 we noticed a regression. I'm not sure if this style should be supported, but I wanted to flag the issue since the previous version handled it without issues.

items.body:
# Feature request

## Summary

<!-- Provide a clear and concise description of the component or enhancement that you want. -->

Currently these properties only allow `string`. If you want to render something like a link inside the title or description then you have to use [custom data](https://base-ui.com/react/components/toast#custom). I don't see a reason why these properties couldn't receive `ReactNode`?

As [discussed on Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1287292451308048406/1423037732212441148).

## Examples in other libraries

- https://sonner.emilkowal.ski/toast#api-reference

items.title: [toast] `promise` method doesn't return toast ID

labels.name: docs
labels.description: Improvements or additions to the documentation.

labels.name: component: toast
labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

labels.name: has workaround
labels.description: There’s a bug, but users have a complete workaround, so no urgent fix or release is needed.

items.body:
https://base-ui.com/react/components/toast#promise-method

The example suggests that `promise` returns the toast ID:

```ts
const toastId = toastManager.promise(
```

However, the types suggest otherwise:

```ts
promise: <Value, Data extends object>(promise: Promise<Value>, options: PromiseOptions<Value, Data>) => Promise<Value>;
```

(It would be useful if it did return the toast ID.)

items.body:
# Bug report ## Current behavior There’s an issue with Toast.Description when providing a custom component via the render prop. Using children as in [the docs](https://base-ui.com/react/components/toast#custom) works, but switching to `render={
...
}` makes the description disappear. Like below (does not show): ```tsx {'`data.userId` is '}{toast.data.userId}} /> ``` If you add `description: "foo"` to the add()'s params, it shows "`data.userId` is 123". But it's not expected behavior. ## Expected behavior Toast.Description should render the provided custom content when passed through the render prop without doing like add({ description: "foo" }). ## Reproducible example https://codesandbox.io/p/sandbox/7slnx9 ## Base UI version v1.0.0-beta.3

labels.name: type: enhancement

labels.description: Introduces changes that are not backward compatible.

labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

items.body:
Closes #1955


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4dd21782-0662-48e8-84eb-240a019b3ad1

## New part

Adds a new `<Toast.Content>` part to apply fade-out and `overflow: hidden` effects to, and to allow `ResizeObserver` to only measure content changes now that the `height` of the toast changes and transitions.

## Styles fixes

There's a new `--peek` variable (user-defined in the demos) and changed `transform` logic that ensures the "peek" part of stacked toasts behind the front one is constant and doesn't change based on the height of the toasts. I also noticed this fixed the animation bug that was present when holding down `Enter` on the toast creator button.

items.title: [toast] Missing type exports

labels.name: component: toast
labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

items.body:
# Bug report

## Current behavior

While building an implementation of `Toast`, I noticed that `ToastObject` is not exposed by the package.

I had to do some bad typing in order to access it: 
```
interface ToastData {
  icon?: React.ReactNode
  design?: 'tinted' | 'filled'
  intent?: 'info' | 'success' | 'warning' | 'error' | 'neutral' | 'surface' | 'surfaceInverse'
  isClosable?: boolean
}

type SparkToast = BaseToast.useToastManager.ReturnValue['toasts'][number] & {
  data: ToastData
}
```

If I don't do this, everything in `toast.data` is typed as `any`.

## Expected behavior

```
import { type ToastObject } from '@base-ui-components/react/toast'

type SparkToast = ToastObject<ToastData>
```

## Base UI version

1.0.0-beta.2

## Which browser are you using?

Chrome

## Which OS are you using?

Mac OS

items.title: [toast][docs] Fix iOS right swipe Tailwind styles

labels.name: docs
labels.description: Improvements or additions to the documentation.

labels.name: component: toast
labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

items.body:
When swiping right on iOS, it should translate to the right, not down

Fixes #2365

---

Another thing I wanted to improve here is the `data-ending-style` translation. I noticed `calc(100% + 4rem)` is much better than `150%` because it works better for any toast height (small or large). Currently tall ones exit too fast, while small ones exit too sluggishly. 

However, it breaks the animation on iOS Safari when quickly creating toasts before the previous one's animation finishes (or rather makes it worse than it is currently). 

Either WebKit borks on the calc or something else is possibly wrong

```diff
&[data-ending-style] {
-  transform: translateY(150%);
+  transform: translateY(calc(100% + 5rem));
}
```

`translateY(100%) translateY(5rem)` also doesn't work

Curious if you had any thoughts/solutions @colmtuite @DiegoAndai @aarongarciah 

Edit: after testing on a real iOS device (not Simulator) the `calc` works fine. Possibly iOS 18.1 (simulator) vs. iOS 18.5 (real) bug

items.title: [toast] Make `Toast.Viewport` an announce container

labels.name: component: toast
labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

items.body:
<!-- Thanks so much for your PR, your contribution is appreciated! ❤️ -->

- [x] I have followed (at least) the [PR section of the contributing guide](https://github.com/mui/base-ui/blob/HEAD/CONTRIBUTING.md#sending-a-pull-request).

Currently the announcer div lives inside the `Toast.Root` as a visually hidden node that has delayed content (since screen readers don't announce content that's inserted with the status node simultaneously, and macOS Safari needs a 'substantial' delay - 50ms was used previously)

This makes the `Viewport` act as the announcer and has some possible benefits:

- Removes timeout/visually hidden hacks.
- Remove `Dialog` `aria-modal` attribute, and instead rely on the `aria-hidden` technique on "outside" elements, which ignores `aria-live` elements (the status announcer).
- For low priority toasts, this change also does announce other internal content, which might not be relevant however
- For high priority toasts, they're rendered in a visually hidden container next to the viewport announce container, as they can receive `role="alert"` when dynamically rendered and avoid being announced multiple times in the status container

items.body:
# Feature request

## Summary

I'd like to make `Toast` appear above/inside a top layer modal `<dialog>`.

This is possible with other components like `Menu` via the `Portal` component. Would it make sense for `Toast` to provide the same?

## Examples in other libraries

None that I'm aware unfortunately.

## Motivation

See above.

items.title: [toast] Support stacking toasts of differing heights

labels.name: type: new feature
labels.description: Expand the scope of the product to solve a new problem.

labels.name: component: toast
labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

items.body:
## Summary

Currently the `expanded` state is forced to be `true` if the heights of the toasts aren't all the same.

https://github.com/mui/base-ui/blob/5d2d1653852c42e4de6e64432b0b76fdcbe12699/packages/react/src/toast/provider/ToastProvider.tsx#L45-L51

This is because if a larger height toast is stacked beneath a smaller height one, it overflows the bottom of it. However there's a way to make it work: minimize larger toasts' height and fade out their content

## Examples in other libraries

https://emilkowal.ski/ui/css-transforms

## Motivation

<!-- What are you trying to accomplish? Which kind of user is this for? Providing context helps us come up with a solution that is more useful in the real world -->

labels.name: breaking change
labels.description: Introduces changes that are not backward compatible.

labels.name: component: toast
labels.description: Changes related to the toast component.

items.body:
Limited toasts were previously removed from the DOM and 'lost to the ether'. 

This change keeps them in the DOM so removing newer toasts lets them come back into view

Preview: https://deploy-preview-1953--base-ui.netlify.app/react/components/toast
Try creating 4+ toasts. When removing a new one it will come back into view.

labels.name: component: toast

items.body:
Im using the react toastify library and when my dialog is open the toast component is beneath the backdrop. Not sure how to go about this but there are certain components that i need to be on top of all the others that may not be a part of base ui library. For some other use cases i used the dialog to overlap another dialog but i can't do the same for this one. A bit of context, this is my layout.tsx: ```
{children}
``` So i think, because of the `.Root` rule anything else is beneath the dialog but is there a way to teleport some other components to the baseui portal?

labels.name: component: toast

items.body: On iOS, the `pointermove` doesn't start firing until swiping ~10px after `pointerdown`. This led to a glitchy jump after swiping past that threshold, which was mentioned by @colmtuite. But the solution to fix this is quite straightforward (as commented), the swipe movement is adjusted from when it starts moving, not where the finger is after swiping
